symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious lower urinary tract symptoms (weak stream, hesitancy, frequency) over a year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Local prostatic tumour growth produces obstructive LUTS; non-specific but the entry point to the prostate story.
LUTS are the classic BPH presentation — the red herring that must not anchor the assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, unremitting rest/night bony low-back and pelvic pain with inability to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest/night bony pain unresponsive to simple analgesia is a hallmark of metastatic bone disease.
Severe progressive spinal pain with loss of mobility is the pre-neurological phase of MSCC.
Degenerative pain is worse with activity and eased by rest — the opposite pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute inability to weight-bear / bedbound for 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute functional collapse from spinal pain mandates urgent imaging to exclude cord compression and pathological fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~6 kg) with anorexia and cachexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss and cachexia point to advanced malignancy rather than a benign cause.
BPH does not cause weight loss or cachexia — its presence pushes decisively away from a benign explanation.
Non-specific but consistent with local prostatic/pelvic disease; adds to the coherent urological picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative neurological deficit screen by history — denies saddle anaesthesia, incontinence, sudden leg weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative history lowers but does NOT exclude early cord compression — definitive exclusion needs in-person neuro exam and MRI, so this cannot be relied upon to stand down urgency.
Non-specific in an unwell elderly patient; adjunct observation, not diagnostic on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, irregular, asymmetrical, nodular prostate on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard irregular nodular gland is characteristic of malignancy vs the smooth symmetrical enlargement of BPH — sealed clinician finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Palpable bladder indicates chronic/partial retention from outlet obstruction — a clinician examination finding.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Delay in confirming and staging prostate malignancy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked PSA elevation with a hard nodular gland strongly supports malignancy — a laboratory test unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skeletal metastases on imaging / adenocarcinoma on prostate biopsy

investigation_details.investigation_name: Whole-spine MRI, bone scan and prostate biopsy
investigation_details.expected_result: osteoblastic bony metastases; adenocarcinoma of the prostate on histology (anticipated)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed cord compression and unconfirmed metastatic disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging plus histology are the confirmatory gold standard — sealed; never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Advanced/metastatic prostate cancer with impending spinal cord compression
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rest/night bony pain + acute inability to weight-bear + unintentional weight loss together define a red-flag presentation demanding urgent in-person assessment — not routine management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude metastatic spinal cord compression — the time-critical diagnosis; required within 24 hours of suspicion. Arranged via urgent in-person/ED pathway, not by the telehealth AI directly.
First-line marker for suspected prostate malignancy.
Assess for anaemia, hypercalcaemia of malignancy and renal impairment from obstruction.
To map skeletal metastatic burden once the acute presentation is stabilised.
Characterises the prostate — but must be performed in person; the telehealth AI must not attempt or simulate it.
